name: Google PageSpeed Insights
description: Run Google's official Lighthouse audit against any URL. Returns full Lighthouse JSON (Performance, Accessibility, SEO, Best Practices, PWA) PLUS real-user Core Web Vitals data from the Chrome User Experience Report (CrUX) when available. Same engine that powers PageSpeed.web.dev. Free with generous rate limits — works without an API key (anonymous, rate-limited) or with a Google Cloud API key for higher quota.

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Google Cloud API key (optional — leave blank for the anonymous tier)

tools.name: run_pagespeed
tools.description: Run Lighthouse + CrUX against a URL and return the full audit. Set strategy='mobile' (default) for the mobile audit or 'desktop' for desktop. Pass `category` once per category you want — performance, accessibility, best-practices, seo, pwa. Default is just performance, which is fastest. Returns the full Lighthouse result JSON nested under `lighthouseResult` and Core Web Vitals under `loadingExperience` (origin-level) and `originLoadingExperience` (URL-level). The agent should pull `lighthouseResult.categories.<cat>.score` for the headline 0-1 score per category, and `lighthouseResult.audits.*` for individual issue details.

strategy.description: Device emulation profile. Mobile is throttled to a slow 4G + mid-tier device; desktop is unthrottled.

category.description: Which Lighthouse categories to run. Each adds latency. Performance only ≈ 15-30s; all five ≈ 30-60s.

locale.description: BCP-47 language code for the audit messages (e.g. 'en', 'fr', 'es', 'ja')

captchaToken.description: reCAPTCHA token if the API requires one (rare)
